questions.question: 1. Which kind of heat treatment is required for making a harder material.

explanations.a: This is an incorrect option. Annealing softens the material
explanations.b: This is the correct option. Quenching is the heat treatment used to increase hardness, whereas annealing results in softening of the material.
explanations.c: This is an incorrect option. Annealing and quenching have opposite effects.
explanations.d: This is an incorrect option. Quenching does make a material harder. Hence, it is incorrect to say “none of them are true”

questions.question: 2. Range of carbon percentage in which hypo eutectic cast iron lies

explanations.a: This is the correct option. This range lies above the steel–cast iron boundary (~2.1% C) and below the eutectic composition (~4.3% C), which defines hypo-eutectic cast iron.
explanations.b: This is an incorrect option. This range is above the eutectic composition and therefore corresponds to hyper-eutectic cast iron, not hypo-eutectic.
explanations.c: This is an incorrect option. This carbon range falls within steels (from eutectoid to upper steel limit), not cast iron.
explanations.d: This is an incorrect option. This range corresponds to low- and medium-carbon steels and is far below the carbon content of cast iron.

explanations.a: This is an incorrect option. This combination represents pearlite constituents after eutectoid transformation, not ledeburite.
explanations.b: This is an incorrect option. This mixture does not occur as an invariant reaction product in the Fe–C phase diagram.
explanations.c: This is the correct option. Ledeburite is the eutectic mixture formed at 4.3 wt.% C and 1147 °C, consisting of austenite and cementite.
explanations.d: This is an incorrect option. Pearlite forms from eutectoid decomposition of austenite, not directly as a eutectic product.

questions.question: 4. State True or False: Ledeburite is a eutectoid mixture of austenite and cementite.

explanations.a: This is an incorrect option. Ledeburite is a eutectic mixture of austenite and cementite, not a eutectoid mixture (the eutectoid mixture is pearlite: ferrite + cementite).
explanations.b: This is the correct option. Ledeburite is a eutectic mixture of austenite and cementite, not a eutectoid mixture (the eutectoid mixture is pearlite: ferrite + cementite).

questions.question: 5. What makes white iron extremely highly wear resistant.

explanations.a: This is an incorrect option. Pearlite is a lamellar mixture of ferrite and cementite and is not hard enough to impart extreme wear resistance.
explanations.b: This is an incorrect option. Austenite is a soft and ductile phase and does not contribute to high wear resistance.
explanations.c: This is the correct option. White iron is highly wear resistant due to the presenceof large amounts of hard and brittle cementite (Fe3C).
explanations.d: This is an incorrect option. Martensite forms by quenching steel, whereas white iron derives its hardness primarily from cementite, not martensite.
